sources_exist.note: All seven canonical HTTP(S) citation occurrences resolve to the named pages or PDFs.

figures_match_sources.note: The citations do not establish the record's two active 541720 consolidators, 5.5x/10.0x social-science/CRM multiples, 25% firm-level adoption, CAPI/SPSS time-to-50% analogs, 42% age-60+ ownership or succession shortage, or the asserted federal/CRM/behavioral-health billing shares.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: The role allocation is complete, contributions sum to the declared 0.30223, and individual fractions are bounded and generally plausible. However, t50_years=7.0 rests on an unrelated historical-analog URL and an adoption source with no 25% 541720 firm rate, while pi_dist=0.25 rests on an unsupported 52% federal cost-reimbursable mix; these are score-affecting judgment defects.

rubric_consistent.note: The BORDERLINE flag is unresolved because S=3.11846842 is only 0.1185 above the 3.00 pass cut and depends on unsupported inputs. The ESTIMATE_HEAVY flag is unresolved because buy_mult, current_adoption_pct, exit_mult and owners_60plus_pct lack evidence at the claimed industry scope. The ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL flag on A, B, M and S is not independently disqualifying, but tolerance-level arithmetic deltas cannot cure unsupported research inputs.

cross_checks_honest.note: All mandatory cross-checks are present and the PRESSURED distinction between statutory fieldwork and replaceable sponsored research is directionally candid. But no_proxy_confirmed cites unsupported CAPI/statistical-software adoption analogs, and pricing_model_deflation repeats an unsubstantiated 52% federal billing share and NICRA mechanism across a mixed for-profit/nonprofit population.

source_audits.note: The February 2023 Riverside release supports PaleoWest's SEAS acquisition as its second add-on and names Commonwealth Heritage Group as a September 2022 add-on. It identifies one historical platform, not two consolidators active in 541720 during the 24 months ending 2026-07-20, and it does not identify the asserted additional platform.

The 2026 sell-side guide gives 5-10x for sub-$10M environmental-services operators and narrower ranges for industrial cleaning, remediation and permit businesses. It provides no 5.5x median for 541720 social-science research or CRM archaeology add-ons and no transaction sample for that population.

The AAPOR report documents survey-research AI use cases, rapid integration of some post-processing tools, and broader US worker-use statistics, but it does not measure 25% material firm-level AI use in NAICS 541720. It also does not contain the quoted 1.6%-to-59% qualitative-research-paper claim.

The source's 8-13x platform range applies to $20M+ environmental-services operators with retainer/MSA revenue and often permit moats; it explicitly centers hazardous waste, remediation, industrial cleaning and emergency response. It does not establish 10.0x for a $10M+ EBITDA social-science research or CRM platform.

The NCSES page is an official report on AI R&D, use and employee impact in the business sector. It contains no CAPI or SPSS discussion and does not support early-1990s-to-2000 CAPI majority adoption, late-1980s SPSS majority adoption, or the claimed 10-15-year time-to-50% analogs.

The April 2026 McKinsey chart supports that over half of all small-business owners are over 55 and roughly six million SMB transitions are expected by 2035. It does not report 42% of 541720 or CRM owners age 60+, 25% age 65+, fewer than one-third with succession plans, or a documented CRM succession shortage.

The guide explains NICRA rate types for nonprofit federal award recipients, including provisional-rate true-ups to actual audited costs and annual proposal timing. It does not establish that 50-55% of 541720 revenue is federal T&M/cost-reimbursable, 25-30% is fixed-price CRM, or 15-20% is behavioral-health work, and it says nothing about per-unit/acre CRM pricing.

reasons: At inputs.active_consolidators (https://www.riversidecompany.com/currents/stratified-environmental-news-release), a 2023 release for one PaleoWest platform and two 2022-2023 add-ons does not support value=2 active consolidators in the last 24 months. Re-run with dated add-on evidence through 2026-07-20 and name every counted 541720/CRM platform.

At inputs.buy_mult and inputs.exit_mult (https://ctacquisitions.com/guides/environmental-services-business-valuation/), environmental-services ranges driven by hazardous-waste permits, remediation and MSA revenue are not comparable evidence for 5.5x and 10.0x social-science/CRM multiples. Re-run with actual 541720 or closely matched CRM transactions/comps, state scale and date, and normalize EBITDA after market-rate owner compensation.

At inputs.current_adoption_pct (https://aapor.org/wp-content/uploads/2026/05/Responsible-AI-Integration-In-Survey-Research.pdf), the report documents use cases but no 25% material firm-level rate for 541720 and no quoted 1.6%-to-59% paper statistic. Re-run with accessible firm-level industry evidence for each anchor and a transparent adjustment to the declared value.

At inputs.historical_analogs (https://ncses.nsf.gov/pubs/nsf25312), the cited NCSES AI report is unrelated to CAPI/SPSS diffusion and contains none of the quoted dates or penetration claims. Re-run with primary or measured adoption-curve evidence supporting time to approximately 50% for each analog before deriving inputs.t50_years.

At inputs.owners_60plus_pct and inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented (https://www.mckinsey.com/featured-insights/week-in-charts/boomers-and-the-business-baton), broad over-55 owner demographics do not support value=0.42 at age 60+ for this industry, and the record expressly says the CRM shortage is inferred rather than documented. Re-run with age-compatible sector evidence and set the succession flag true only with explicit shortage evidence.

At inputs.pricing_structure and inputs.pi_dist (https://grantsights.com/blog/indirect-cost-rate-negotiation-guide), a nonprofit NICRA explainer does not establish the score-affecting 541720 federal/CRM/behavioral-health billing mix or that NICRA applies as described to the dominant for-profit evaluation contractors. Re-run with contract-award or industry revenue evidence for each billing share and distinguish grants, T&M, cost-reimbursement and fixed-price treatment before deriving pi_dist.

At cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed and cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation, the record presents the unsupported CAPI/SPSS analogs and 52% federal billing mix as established facts. Re-run the cross-checks after replacing the cited inputs, and state only mechanisms and shares directly supported for the scored dominant sub-segment.
flags_reviewed: ESTIMATE_HEAVY: 4 inputs with quality ESTIMATE (buy_mult, current_adoption_pct, exit_mult, owners_60plus_pct)
ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on A, B, M, S
